evidences.title: Saints face brutal Super Bowl odds next season with a steep climb ...
evidences.content: With Super Bowl LX said and done, all eyes are on the next big game. The Los Angeles Rams and Seattle Seahawks are the odds-on favorites to appear, with odds of +950. Currently, the New Orleans Saints are on the other end of the spectrum with 100-1 odds. That's tied for third worst in the league with the New York Giants, Atlanta Falcons, Carolina Panthers, and Tennessee Titans. However, there is still a visible path to the Super Bowl when looking at the numbers. The Saints have the second-best odds at winning their division, +350, which gives them an automatic playoff berth. Only last year's division champions, the Tampa Bay Buccaneers, have higher odds (+120). [...] It's clear by the way that the odds are set that the Saints are underestimated heading into next season. It's understandable in a way, because New Orleans did finish last in their division this past season. Their roster next season, on paper, is also looking to be worse than last season's. It's important to note that it is way too early to tell what the final 53-man roster will look like, but with the players on the roster right now, that's the unfortunate case. ## The Saints are expected to face an uphill battle in...

evidences.title: New Orleans Saints have already been eliminated from the playoffs
evidences.content: We knew this year was going to be a rebuild. But even top decision-makers in New Orleans couldn't have anticipated this. They should have. The Saints missing the playoffs this year means longtime general manager Mickey Loomis has never built a playoff team in eight years without Sean Payton coaching it. Between his four years running the front office from 2002 to 2005 and the four years since Payton left the team from 2022 to 2025, none of those eight teams have qualified for the postseason. [...] # The New Orleans Saints have already been eliminated from the playoffs It isn't even December (at least not at the the final whistle in Sunday's game), but the New Orleans Saints have already been eliminated from playoffs contention. First-year head coach Kellen Moore has steered the team into a 2-10 rut in 2025, the worst start for a rookie coach since the Saints' inaugural 1967 season, where they finished 3-14. Because the Saints lost to the Miami Dolphins while the Carolina Panthers upset the Los Angeles Rams, New Orleans was mathematically eliminated from being able to win enough games to close the gap in the NFC South. The Tampa Bay Buccaneers beat the Arizona Cardinals, too, whi...

evidences.content: Oddsmakers took note of the strong finish. Year over year, New Orleans’ preseason win total rose from 6.5 to 7.5, tied for second amongst NFC South teams. Here’s a look at the NFL odds for New Orleans’ 2025 win total and my Saints win total prediction. ## Saints Win Total Odds The Saints’ season win total is set at 7.5 games. Based on the opening odds, New Orleans’ outcome against the win total is viewed as a coin flip. Currently, the Saints are -130 to finish over 7.5 wins. Moore’s team is simultaneously +110 to go under 7.5 wins this season. If New Orleans finishes above this benchmark, it will mark the second straight year the Saints have cleared their season win total. Here are the 2026 NFL win totals for all NFC South teams: [...] Here are the 2026 NFL win totals for all NFC South teams: Tampa Bay Buccaneers: 8.5 Wins Atlanta Falcons: 7.5 Wins New Orleans Saints: 7.5 Wins Carolina Panthers: 6.5 Wins ## Saints Win Total Prediction 2026 Based on New Orleans’ easy schedule and sustained defensive success from the end of last season, I predict New Orleans finishes Over 7.5 Wins (-110). evidences.title: 2026 NFL draft order: Saints take a step back after Week 13 loss
evidences.content: Saints Wire Saints Wire # 2026 NFL draft order: Saints take a step back after Week 13 loss ## It goes from bad to worse. The New Orleans Saints lost this week and got mathematically eliminated from playoff contention, but that's not all. They also took a step back in the 2026 draft order. Because the New York Giants lost in a blowout to the New England Patriots on Monday night, Big Blue falls to 2-11 on the year, one loss ahead of the Saints at 2-10. That's the difference in picking second or third overall. [...] Of course it isn't over yet. Another Saints loss this week to the Tampa Bay Buccaneers, while the Giants are on bye, would put New Orleans in the No. 2 spot behind the one-win Tennessee Titans. But we still need to keep an eye on the 2-10 Las Vegas Raiders, too. While it's looking more and more likely the Saints will be picking top-five next year, it isn't a sure thing just yet. For now, here are the top 10 picks in 2026, ordered by the experts at Tankathon: [...] If you're curious, running the table would mean the Saints pick no higher than 15th in the upcoming draft class. They're capped at seven wins and there are too many seven-win teams competing for a playoff bert...

evidences.title: The New Orleans Saints have already been eliminated from the ...
evidences.content: It isn't even December (at least not at the the final whistle in Sunday's game), but the New Orleans Saints have already been eliminated from playoffs contention. First-year head coach Kellen Moore has steered the team into a 2-10 rut in 2025, the worst start for a rookie coach since the Saints' inaugural 1967 season, where they finished 3-14. Because the Saints lost to the Miami Dolphins while the Carolina Panthers upset the Los Angeles Rams, New Orleans was mathematically eliminated from being able to win enough games to close the gap in the NFC South. The Tampa Bay Buccaneers beat the Arizona Cardinals, too, which also would have eliminated the Saints. [...] We knew this year was going to be a rebuild. But even top decision-makers in New Orleans couldn't have anticipated this. They should have. The Saints missing the playoffs this year means longtime general manager Mickey Loomis has never built a playoff team in eight years without Sean Payton coaching it. Between his four years running the front office from 2002 to 2005 and the four years since Payton left the team from 2022 to 2025, none of those eight teams have qualified for the postseason. evidences.content: # What NFL teams play on Super Bowl Sunday tonight? Who is in SB LX? Time, channel, streaming, more Super Bowl 2026 is officially here, but who closes out the final game of the NFL playoffs? What team will meet for the Lombardi Trophy in Super Bowl LX? Here's to know about how to watch the NFL playoffs, the current 2026 NFL Draft order, and more: ## Who is in 2026 Super Bowl LX? What teams play on Super Bowl Sunday? The Seattle Seahawks and the New England Patriots will meet in Super Bowl LX − a rematch of Super Bowl XLIX. The Seahawks held off the Los Angeles Rams, 31-27, to claim the NFC Championship on Jan. 25, while the Patriots defeated the Denver Broncos, 10-7, to win the AFC Championship earlier in the day. ## When is 2026 Super Bowl LX? What is Super Bowl 60 date? evidences.title: NFL Super Bowl LX schedule: The Big Game Matchup is Set
evidences.content: The Seattle Seahawks took care of their division rivals, the Los Angeles Rams, and got to celebrate the NFC Championship in front of their fans. This was a back and forth game as expected, but the Seahawks came out on top, stopping the Rams short on the final drive of the game. The Seahawks have been the favorites to win the Super Bowl since the start of the playoffs, and that hasn’t changed as the matchup is officially set. ## Super Bowl LX: February 8th Seattle Seahawks (1) vs New England Patriots (2) Sunday, 6:30 p.m. ET on NBC, Peacock, Telemundo FanDuel odds: Seahawks -4.5, 46.5 O/U Seattle -230 New England +190 ## More in: Washington Commanders vs Philadelphia Eagles 2025: Everything you need to know for the NFC title game [...] Community Logo Community Logo # NFL Super Bowl LX schedule: The Big Game Matchup is Set Super Bowl! We had two big games for the conference championship games, and we now know who’s going to play in Super Bowl LX. The Seattle Seahawks will be facing the New England Patriots in a rematch of Super Bowl XLIX. The Patriots punched their ticket to the big game by defeating the Denver Broncos in their house. The Broncos were starting backup QB Jarrett St...

evidences.title: Morning Break: Saints prepare for final matchup - New Orleans Saints
evidences.content: For all he knows, this could be Cam Jordan's final time playing his most hated rival as a member of the New Orleans Saints. The Saints, who were eliminated from playoff contention way back in November, will play their final game of the 2025 season Sunday against the Atlanta Falcons. SEE MORE>> Additional News Jeff Duncan’s crystal ball for the 2026 Louisiana sports year ### Saints News from NewOrleansSaints.com Saints Wednesday Injury Report: 2025 Week 18 vs. Atlanta Falcons 13 Saints listed on Wednesday's Saints Injury Report for Week 18 against the Falcons. evidences.title: How does the story end for Saints? | Multimedia - NOLA.com
evidences.content: site-logo site-logo site-logo # How does the story end for Saints? The New Orleans Saints play their 2025 season finale Sunday against the Atlanta Falcons, and our Saints Insider crew is here to break it down. We'll analyze the matchups in the game, the significant injuries the Saints are dealing with and the stakes of this game, which is plenty meaningful even though both teams have been eliminated from playoff contention. Can the Saints win their fifth in a row to end the season? We'll make our predictions, too. #saints #neworleanssaints #whodat #whodatnation #falcons #atlantafalcons #riseup #nfl #nflweek18 #nfldraft #nflplayoffs #football NOLA.com # #### Sections NOLA.com # #### Services #### Our Sites #### Contact Information News Tips: nolanewstips@theadvocate.com [...] News Tips: nolanewstips@theadvocate.com Other questions: subscriberservices@theadvocate.com Need help? #### Browser Compatibility Your browser is out of date and potentially vulnerable to security risks.
